steps.title: Identify the independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses. The first, “Before the park's construction, the surface temperature 30 meters outside of the park boundary was 0.1°C cooler than that of a control area,” is complete and can stand alone. The second, “the temperature 30 meters outside of the boundary was 1.7°C cooler than that of the control area,” is also a complete independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the sentence boundary rule
steps.content:
Choice C places a period after “area,” properly separating two independent clauses into distinct sentences. “After construction” in the second sentence provides a temporal contrast to “Before” in the first. Choices A and D join the independent clauses with a comma alone, creating a comma splice, while choice B is structurally incomplete.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
